info.name: EcoRoute Engine API
info.description: Ready-to-run Postman collection for EcoRoute Engine. Run the folders in order from top to bottom after the backend is running and the database has been migrated. The collection self-seeds a unique organization, admin user, business, vehicle, pickup requests, dispatch assignment, route, dashboard checks, and audit-log checks. Default base URL: http://localhost:3000.

item.name: 00 - Service Discovery
item.description: Public service discovery, health, and API documentation endpoints.

request.description: Returns service metadata and endpoint map. The application exposes this at both `/` and `api/v1`.
Versioned API index.
Returns backend, database, and OSRM health information. OSRM may be reported as `skipped` in offline mode.
Swagger UI HTML endpoint for the generated OpenAPI documentation.

exec: pm.test('Swagger UI returns success or redirect', function () {

item.name: 01 - Auth
item.description: Authentication flow. Run `Register Organization and Admin` first; it creates a unique account and stores tokens automatically.

request.description: Creates a new organization and admin user. Saves access token, refresh token, user ID, and organization ID.
Authenticates the admin user and refreshes collection token variables.
Uses the refresh token to obtain a new access and refresh token pair.

item.name: 02 - Businesses
item.description: Business location management. Requires admin or dispatcher token.

request.description: Creates a business pickup location and stores `businessId`.
Returns paginated businesses for the authenticated organization.
Fetches the business created by this collection run.

exec: pm.test('Returned business matches captured ID', function () {

item.name: 03 - Vehicles
item.description: Vehicle management and status operations. Requires admin for create and driver assignment; admin or dispatcher for read/status.

request.description: Creates an available vehicle and stores `vehicleId`.
Lists vehicles with optional status filter.
Fetches the vehicle created by this collection run.

exec: pm.test('Returned vehicle matches captured ID', function () {

request.description: Clears the driver assignment. This is safe for a self-contained collection because no driver seed endpoint exists.

item.name: 04 - Pickups
item.description: Pickup request lifecycle. Creates one pickup for dispatch and one independent pickup for cancellation testing.

body.raw:
{
  "businessId": "{{businessId}}",
  "wasteCategory": "plastic",
  "estimatedWeightKg": 120,
  "scheduledAt": "2026-06-12T09:30:00.000Z",
  "notes": "Generated by Postman collection for dispatch flow"
}

request.description: Creates a pending pickup that will be assigned to the created vehicle. Stores `pickupId`.

body.raw:
{
  "businessId": "{{businessId}}",
  "wasteCategory": "paper",
  "estimatedWeightKg": 40,
  "scheduledAt": "2026-06-12T10:30:00.000Z",
  "notes": "Generated by Postman collection for cancellation flow"
}

request.description: Creates a second pending pickup used by the cancellation endpoint. Stores `cancelPickupId`.
Lists pickup requests with optional filters for status, vehicle ID, and business ID.
Runs a PostGIS proximity search for nearby pickup requests.

item.name: Get My Pickups - Driver Endpoint RBAC Check

request.description: Driver-only endpoint for assigned pickups. The self-seeded collection uses an admin token because there is no public driver creation endpoint, so this request verifies RBAC and expects 403.

exec: pm.test('Admin token is forbidden from driver-only endpoint', function () {

request.description: Fetches the dispatch pickup by ID.

exec: pm.test('Returned pickup matches captured ID', function () {

request.description: Valid status transition from pending to cancelled.

item.name: 05 - Dispatch and Routes
item.description: Capacity-checked dispatch assignment and route lifecycle. Run after creating vehicle and pickup resources.

request.description: Assigns the dispatch pickup to the vehicle. Verifies capacity and prevents duplicate assignment.

exec: pm.test('Vehicle is dispatched and pickup is assigned', function () {

request.description: Generates an active route for the dispatched vehicle. With `OFFLINE_MODE=true`, the backend uses its local OSRM stub. With external OSRM enabled, local OSRM must be reachable.
Lists routes for the organization with optional vehicle/status filters.
Fetches the generated route by ID.

exec: pm.test('Returned route matches captured ID', function () {

request.description: Completes the active route, marks assigned pickups completed, and resets the vehicle to available.
Updates vehicle status after the route is completed and vehicle load is reset.
Returns aggregate operational summary metrics for the organization.
Returns vehicle workload and utilization metrics.

exec: pm.test('Vehicle workload response is array', function () {

request.description: Returns pickup status statistics.

exec: pm.test('Pickup stats response has expected shape', function () {

request.description: Lists organization-scoped audit logs. Requires admin role.
Protected endpoint without bearer token. Expected response: 401.
Invalid payload test. Expected response: 400 validation error.
Replays the same dispatch assignment after the pickup is already assigned/completed. Expected response: 409 conflict.

exec: pm.test('Duplicate dispatch returns conflict or invalid state', function () {
